_comment: What the STUDY subagent (vr-arch) emits each round: ONLY what it newly learned or needs to correct — never the whole model. vr_arch_merge.py integrates this into out/arch_model.json (add/refine/retract) and re-renders out/architecture.md. Use op='add' for new knowledge, op='refine' to correct/deepen an existing entry by id (e.g. analysis proved an earlier note wrong), op='retract' to withdraw an entry shown false, op='answer' to close an open_question. Corrections are recorded in each entry's history (never silently overwritten).

components.name: CSF command queue
components.lifecycle: create via ioctl -> bind -> mmap doorbell -> destroy

components.note: analysis found bounds check at L88; earlier 'no validation' was wrong

optimizations.name: lockless ring buffer
optimizations.technique: producer/consumer head/tail indices, no lock on fast path
optimizations.risk_notes: head/tail must be validated against ring size on each use

optimizations.note: no custom allocator after all — uses standard kmalloc

open_questions.question: Is the doorbell page user-mapped while the queue can be freed?

open_questions.note: yes, mmap'd in kbase_csf_mmap; lifetime tied to ctx
